title: Column-Major Memory Layout — Cache Lines Are Everything
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Column-major layout provides 4-8× throughput advantage over row-major for numerical hot paths due to cache line coalescing
content.proof: Fortran stores arrays column-major by default. Sequential access along columns = sequential memory = cache line hits. The CPU prefetcher can predict and stream. Row-major traversal on column-major data = cache line thrashing.
content.benchmark: Column-major batch: 2.27B ops/sec. Row-major equivalent: ~340M ops/sec on same hardware
content.caveat: Only matters for large arrays that exceed L2 cache. Small arrays fit regardless.
